first_pass_gold_use: Exclude canceled suspected-fraud and shipping-canceled records from primary AO1 Gold; retain only for audit sensitivity or descriptive dashboard outputs
excluded_or_deferred_fields: Delivery_Status = Shipping canceled; Order_Status = CANCELED; Order_Status = SUSPECTED_FRAUD
rationale: AO1 should model late-delivery risk for normal fulfilled or fulfillment-eligible orders rather than cancellation or fraud outcomes
future_action: Sensitivity analysis may compare inclusion versus exclusion if needed

first_pass_gold_use: Include Type as an order-creation candidate feature with caveats
excluded_or_deferred_fields: Remove or reclassify if later evidence shows Type is generated after payment review fraud review or order processing
team_assumption: Type is assumed to represent payment transaction type selected or recorded at order creation: TRANSFER direct bank transfer; DEBIT debit card payment; CASH cash-on-hand payment; PAYMENT other primarily credit-based payment types
rationale: Under the documented assumption Type is decision-time valid but metadata is not fully explicit
future_action: Review unusually strong model effects during interpretation

first_pass_gold_use: Use Order_Item_Total as AO3 predicted-margin denominator
excluded_or_deferred_fields: Exclude Order_Item_Total from primary AO2 predictors
rationale: Order_Item_Total is useful for AO3 margin but closely related to price quantity discount sales and profit
future_action: A later AO2 sensitivity model may revisit it

first_pass_gold_use: Use minimal commercial predictors: Order_Item_Product_Price; Order_Item_Discount_Rate; Order_Item_Quantity
excluded_or_deferred_fields: Order_Item_Total; Sales; Sales_per_customer; Order_Item_Discount; Product_Price; Order_Item_Profit_Ratio; Benefit_per_order; Order_Profit_Per_Order
rationale: One price field one discount-rate field and one quantity field reduce duplicate economic inputs and target-reconstruction risk
future_action: Alternative commercial specifications may be tested later as sensitivity models

first_pass_gold_use: Use product category and product department
excluded_or_deferred_fields: Exclude Product_Name product_name_normalized product IDs product catalog keys and direct product-level identifiers
rationale: Direct product-level fields are high-cardinality potentially unstable and less interpretable for first-pass Gold
future_action: Consider grouped product features later using top-N grouping frequency thresholds or category hierarchy

first_pass_gold_use: Use coarse geography such as Market Order_Region Order_Country Customer_Country and Customer_Segment
excluded_or_deferred_fields: Exclude Customer_City Order_City postal codes precise coordinates granular region keys and city-region composite keys
rationale: Granular geography is often high-cardinality missing-heavy unstable or too granular for a conservative first-pass model
future_action: Consider grouping binning or top-N geography rules later

first_pass_gold_use: Do not include historical aggregates in first model-ready Gold tables
excluded_or_deferred_fields: Customer/product/city/region/category historical aggregates such as late-delivery rates profitability averages delay rates and discount behavior
rationale: Historical aggregates can be useful but require strict chronological split awareness and train-only computation to prevent leakage
future_action: Create a future issue with smoothing missing-history frequency-threshold and test-period handling requirements
